description: Ready-to-use LogQL recipes for ad-hoc investigation in Explore. Each panel shows the query and its purpose. Open the panel's query in Explore to run it interactively.

options.content:
## Ion Explore Cookbook

This dashboard contains ready-to-run LogQL recipes for ad-hoc investigation. **Use it as a launching pad into Explore**, not as a live monitoring dashboard.

### How to use a recipe
1. Set the dashboard variables above (Conversation ID, Session ID, Extension Name) to scope the queries.
2. Click the **three-dot menu** (⋮) on any panel and select **Explore** to open that query in Grafana Explore.
3. In Explore you can adjust the time range, modify the query, and use **split-view** to correlate logs with traces.

### Key fields (structured metadata promoted by Alloy)
| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `context_conversation_id` | Durable conversation-file ID — join key across all event types |
| `context_session_id` | Engine session key (tab UUID for desktop clients) |
| `context_extension` | Hosting extension name (omit-when-absent for non-extension runs) |
| `context_extension_version` | Extension manifest version (omit-when-absent) |
| `trace_id` | 32-hex trace ID — click the Tempo correlation link to jump to the trace |
| `model` | LLM model name (on llm.call, run.complete) |
| `run_cost_usd` | Per-run cost in USD (on run.complete) |

### Provisioned correlations
The Loki datasource has three provisioned correlations: **conversation_id → all logs**, **session_id → telemetry**, **trace_id → Tempo trace**. In any Explore result, click the link button on a log line's field value to follow the correlation.

panels.title: All logs for a conversation
panels.description: Every log line across all components (engine, extensions, desktop, iOS) that carries this conversation_id. Set the 'Conversation ID' variable above. This is the primary first-look query for any reported bug.

panels.title: Telemetry events for a conversation
panels.description: Only telemetry events (run.complete, llm.call, dispatch.agent, cache.savings) for the given conversation_id. Use this to audit cost and timing for a single conversation without log noise.

panels.title: All telemetry for a session
panels.description: All telemetry events (run.complete, llm.call, dispatch.agent, cache.savings) for the given session_id (the engine session key / tab UUID). Useful for session-level cost forensics when you know the tab/session but not the conversation ID.

panels.title: All runs attributed to an extension
panels.description: All run.complete events where context_extension matches the Extension Name variable. Useful for reviewing all runs (cost, model, turns) driven by a specific extension. Old runs without context_extension are excluded — they appear as 'unattributed' in the Extensions dashboard.

panels.title: Agent dispatches attributed to an extension
panels.description: All dispatch.agent spans where context_extension matches. Shows which sub-agents the extension dispatched, at what depth, and with what model. Useful for understanding sub-agent cost within an extension.

panels.title: LLM calls with trace IDs
panels.description: All llm.call telemetry events that carry a trace_id. Click the 'View trace' correlation link on any result row (via the provisioned Loki→Tempo correlation) to jump directly to the Tempo trace for that call.

panels.title: Engine errors
panels.description: All ERROR-level log lines from the engine. Start here for bug triage — every engine error path logs at ERROR level with structured fields. Filter further by conversation_id or session_id after identifying the relevant event.

panels.title: Extension errors
panels.description: All ERROR-level log lines from extensions (component=extension). Cross-reference with the extension name via the 'tag' field (e.g. tag=ion-dev). Useful for debugging extension panics and unhandled hook rejections.

panels.title: Ingest freshness by component (min since last line)
panels.description: Minutes since the most recent log line per component — the tailer-wedge detector. When one component's tile climbs while the others stay near zero, that file's Alloy cursor is wedged (frozen positions offset against a still-growing file — see README "Tailer wedge"). Diagnosis: compare the positions offset in the container against the host file size; fix with `docker compose -p ion-obs restart alloy`. The [24h] lookback keeps a long-wedged component visible as a growing red value instead of dropping it from a narrow window.

targets.expr: (vector(${__to:date:seconds}) - on() group_right() max by (component) (max_over_time({component=~".+"} | label_format ts_unix="{{ __timestamp__ | unixEpoch }}" | unwrap ts_unix [24h]))) / 60

list.name: conversation_id
list.label: Conversation ID
list.description: Paste a conversation ID ({millis}-{hex12}) to filter panels to a single conversation.

list.name: session_id
list.label: Session ID
list.description: Paste an engine session key (tab UUID or equivalent) to filter panels to a single session.

list.name: extension
list.label: Extension Name
list.description: Extension name (e.g. 'ion-dev') to scope extension-attribution panels.

title: Ion Explore Cookbook
